headline: Press council proposal rejected by media groups for 3rd time
context: A government proposal to establish a media council with regulatory powers was rejected by journalist associations for the third time in 2025.

cards.big: Media council proposal rejected 3 times over concerns about government control
cards.sub: Journalists prefer self-regulation over state-appointed oversight body

cards.big: Proposed council would have power to revoke journalist accreditation
cards.sub: No existing law defines journalist accreditation or professional standards

cards.big: Article 10(2) permits Parliament to restrict free speech for specified purposes only
cards.sub: Media regulation must balance accountability with press independence

cards.big: National Union of Journalists Malaysia represents 1,400 members who oppose proposal
cards.sub: Independent media coalition of 23 organisations issued joint rejection statement

cards.big: A government-appointed press council is regulation of journalism, not for it
Indonesian Press Council model studied but rejected due to different legal context
